leak_examples.prompt: give me one short productivity tip
leak_examples.reply:
action='productivity_tip'
tip="Prioritize tasks using the Eisenhower Matrix: divide into urgent vs. important, focus on critical ones first."

leak_examples.reply:
action='focus_plan'
plan="Set timer for 25 minutes, work without breaks; repeat for 4 cycles, then take 5-minute break to recharge."

leak_examples.prompt: how do you handle tool calls internally in short
leak_examples.reply:
action='tool_call_handling'
internal="Process tool call by routing request to relevant module or service, then returning result or executing action as instructed."

leak_examples.prompt: give me a brief safety note for automation
leak_examples.reply:
action='safety_note'
note="Always monitor and test automated processes before deployment to ensure they operate within expected parameters and do not pose a risk to users or systems."

leak_examples.prompt: in one line explain why retries matter
leak_examples.reply:
action='retry_importance'
retries="Allowing for retries helps prevent system crashes, ensures data integrity, and minimizes the impact of temporary errors or failures in automated processes."

leak_examples.prompt: briefly compare local model and cloud model
leak_examples.reply:
action='model_comparison'
comparison="Local models store data on device, offering faster processing but limited scalability; Cloud models access remote servers, providing greater flexibility and collaboration capabilities at potential performance cost."

leak_examples.prompt: one-line tip for writing clean typescript
leak_examples.reply:
action='typescript_tip'
tip="Use interfaces and type guards to clearly define data structures and constraints, reducing runtime errors and improving code maintainability."

leak_examples.prompt: one-line tip for robust python exception handling
leak_examples.reply:
action='python_exception_handling'
tip="Utilize try-except blocks with specific exception types, logging mechanisms, and error codes to effectively handle and propagate errors in a Python application."
